team_context.competitive_window: building — young franchise QB on rookie deal, aggressive offseason spending, but coming off 5-12 disaster season
team_context.cap_situation: flush — ~$76M in cap space (most in NFL per OTC), spending aggressively in 2026 FA on Oweh ($100M), Tunsil extension ($60.2M), Chenal, Robertson, Okonkwo, etc.
team_context.trade_posture: Net buyers building around Jayden Daniels' rookie contract window. Willing to move declining veterans or players who underperformed in 2025 to stockpile future assets while spending cap dollars in free agency. Have 7th overall pick and 6 total draft picks.

players.name: Jayden Daniels
players.performance_evidence: 2024 Offensive Rookie of the Year: 3,568 pass yards, 25 TD, 9 INT plus 891 rush yards and 6 rush TD. Led Commanders to NFC Championship Game. 2025 derailed by injuries — played only 7 games (knee sprain, hamstring strain, dislocated elbow). 1,262 pass yards, 8 TD, 3 INT in limited action. Talent is elite-tier but health is a concern entering Year 3.

trade_value.reasoning: Franchise quarterback on a rookie deal with 3 years of control remaining at ~$10M/year cap hit. Even with 2025 injury concerns, a 25-year-old former OROY on this contract is the single most valuable asset type in the NFL. Worth multiple 1st-round picks in any realistic trade scenario.

players.availability_reasoning: The entire franchise is built around Daniels. Peters, Quinn, and ownership have been explicit that he is the centerpiece. They invested $60M in Tunsil's extension specifically to protect him. Zero chance of trade under any circumstances.
players.injury_notes: Missed 10 games in 2025 with knee sprain (Wk 2), hamstring strain (Wk 7), and dislocated left elbow (Wk 9, aggravated Wk 14). Shut down for final 3 games. No surgery required. Durability is the single biggest question mark entering 2026.

players.name: Terry McLaurin
players.performance_evidence: 2024: 82 receptions, 1,096 yards, franchise-record 13 TD. 2nd-team All-Pro, 2x Pro Bowl. 2025: Injury-limited to 10 games — 38 rec, 582 yards, 3 TD. Still graded 9th among WRs by PFF (86.5) when on field. Five consecutive 1,000-yard seasons from 2020-2024. Signed 3-year, $97M extension in August 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite WR1 production when healthy, but massive dead cap ($42.5M) makes him essentially untradeable in 2026. Contract structure suggests 2026 could be his last year in Washington per multiple analysts. If trade were somehow structured, the talent warrants a late 1st, but financial constraints make it near-impossible.

players.availability_reasoning: Dead cap of $42.5M if traded in 2026 makes this financially impossible. McLaurin is Daniels' primary weapon. Multiple reports suggest 2026 could be his final year in Washington based on contract structure, but that's a 2027 discussion, not a 2026 one.
players.injury_notes: Missed 7 games in 2025 with a quad injury. Played only 10 games — career low.
players.trade_buzz: Matthew Berry and others have noted 2026 is 'likely' McLaurin's last year with the Commanders based on contract structure. This is a future storyline, not a current trade scenario.

players.name: Luke McCaffrey
players.performance_evidence: 2025: 18 receptions for 168 yards in 17 games. Showed flashes as kick returner (29.9 yards/return avg). Former college QB still developing as a WR. Coaches noted 'night and day' improvement from Year 1 to Year 2. Placed on IR late in 2025 season.

trade_value.reasoning: Raw, developing WR on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control. High upside due to athleticism (6-2, 200, 4.4 speed) and football IQ from playing QB, but production hasn't materialized yet. Worth a late-round pick swap at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Commanders need WR help and may draft or sign a WR2. McCaffrey could become expendable if a better option emerges. However, his cheap contract and upside mean Washington would want fair value.

players.name: Jaylin Lane
players.performance_evidence: 2025 rookie: Made contributions as a depth receiver, mentioned alongside other key rookies as a positive development for the team. Still developing in the offense.

trade_value.reasoning: Day 3 rookie WR on a cheap deal with 4 years of control. Minimal proven production but maximum cost-effectiveness. Not worth much in trade but also not worth giving away.

players.availability_reasoning: Cheap rookie contract with 4 years of control. Commanders need receiver depth and wouldn't move him without good reason.

players.name: Chig Okonkwo
players.performance_evidence: Signed as FA from Tennessee in March 2026. Proven 'move' TE who can win in the slot and outside. Solid blocker. Runs well after the catch. Not a big red zone target historically but provides an ability the Commanders' TE room lacked.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, versatile TE on a new 3-year deal just signed in free agency. Commanders specifically targeted this skill set for Daniels. Would cost an early-to-mid 3rd to pry away, but Washington just signed him to fill a specific need.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed in free agency to fill a specific need. Washington wouldn't move him unless blown away by an offer. High dead cap in Year 1 of the deal also discourages trades.

players.name: Ben Sinnott
players.performance_evidence: Through 2 NFL seasons: 8 receptions on 8 targets, 2 TD, 62.5% success rate. Developing as a blocker. Limited pass-catching opportunities behind Ertz/Bates in 2024-25. Beat writers project a potential Year 3 breakout in 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 2nd-round pick on a cheap rookie deal with upside, but minimal NFL production to date. The pedigree and contract make him worth a mid-round pick, but the lack of production caps the value.

players.availability_reasoning: With Okonkwo signed, Sinnott's path to meaningful snaps is less clear. Commanders may be willing to move him for fair value if they get a good offer, especially with a new offensive coordinator potentially changing personnel preferences.

players.name: Jacory Croskey-Merritt
players.performance_evidence: 2025 rookie: 175 carries, 805 rushing yards (4.6 YPC), 8 TD (2nd-most among rookie RBs). PFF rushing grade 75.4. Led team in rushing despite committee approach. Had multiple 100-yard games including 105 yards and 2 TD vs Dallas on Christmas. 7th-round steal.

trade_value.reasoning: Productive rookie RB on a minimum-level contract with 4 years of control. RB position value is low, which caps the pick equivalent. But cost-controlled, young, productive RBs on late-round deals have real value — worth a 4th-round pick equivalent.

players.availability_reasoning: Commanders are likely to target RB in the draft (Jeremiyah Love linked at #7 overall). If they draft a RB high, Croskey-Merritt becomes trade-able. Until then, he's their RB1 on an incredibly cheap deal.

players.name: Marcus Mariota
players.performance_evidence: 2025: Started 10 games when Daniels was injured. 63.2% completion, 1,359 yards, 9 TD, 6 INT. Reliable veteran backup. Re-signed on 1-year, $7M deal for 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: Veteran backup QB with starting experience on a 1-year deal. Some value as injury insurance for a contender, but the $7M cap hit limits interest. Worth a conditional late-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Washington just re-signed him specifically to back up Daniels. They wouldn't move him unless they had a replacement lined up, but a mid-season trade to a QB-needy contender is plausible if Daniels stays healthy.

players.name: Laremy Tunsil
players.performance_evidence: 5x Pro Bowl LT. Just signed record-setting 2-year, $60.2M extension ($30.1M/yr AAV, highest-paid OL in NFL). Played 14 games in 2025 and was one of Washington's best players. ESPN ranked him 7th among OTs. Some penalty concerns (19 in 2024). Signed through 2028.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite blindside protector for franchise QB, but age (32) and massive dead cap ($43.3M) make him completely untradeable financially. If trade were somehow possible, the talent and contract length command a late 1st.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a record-breaking extension. Dead cap is $43.3M. Washington traded a massive haul (2nd, 3rd, 4th, 7th round picks) to acquire him from Houston and then extended him. He is the cornerstone of Daniels' protection.
players.injury_notes: Missed 3 games in 2025. Generally durable across career.

players.name: Josh Conerly Jr.
players.performance_evidence: 2025 rookie: Started all 17 games at RT. PFF grade 59.9 overall — rough start but improved significantly down the stretch (71.6 PFF grade from Weeks 9-18, allowed only 2 sacks). PFF named him the team's top breakout candidate for 2026. Developmental prospect with high ceiling.

trade_value.reasoning: 22-year-old 1st-round OT on a fully guaranteed rookie deal with 4 years of control. Showed improvement trajectory in 2025. Young tackles with his pedigree and contract are extremely valuable even with a rough rookie year. Worth a 2nd-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Drafted specifically to be the long-term RT next to Tunsil. Just 22 years old with improving play. Washington invested the 29th overall pick in him and he's part of the offensive line core being built around Daniels.

players.name: Sam Cosmi
players.performance_evidence: Signed 4-year, $74M extension. Returned from ACL tear and played well in the second half of 2025. Previously a Pro Bowl-caliber guard. Spotrac notes he is 'guaranteed through 2026 and a fixture on the right side of the OL.' Considered a core piece of the offensive line.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old guard on a long-term deal with Pro Bowl upside. However, $28M dead cap makes him untradeable in practical terms. If the financial constraint didn't exist, he'd be worth a high 2nd.

players.availability_reasoning: Guaranteed through 2026 with $28M dead cap if traded. Core piece of the OL. Washington is building a long-term offensive line around Daniels, and Cosmi is part of that foundation.
players.injury_notes: Tore ACL in 2024, returned during 2025 season. Missed first 6 games of 2025 recovering. Played well upon return.

players.name: Nick Allegretti
players.performance_evidence: 2025: Reduced to part-time player after being full-time starter in 2024. 3x Super Bowl champion with Kansas City. Signed extension during 2026 FA period. Versatile G/C. Provides interior depth and veteran stability.

trade_value.reasoning: Veteran interior lineman with championship experience on a modest deal. Reduced role in 2025 limits value. With Chris Paul leaving in FA, Allegretti's role may increase, but he's a depth piece worth a 5th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Just extended but at modest money. Washington may draft an interior OL or sign one. If they find an upgrade, Allegretti becomes moveable at fair value. Low dead cap makes this financially feasible.

players.name: Andrew Wylie
players.performance_evidence: Started 14 games at RT in 2024, moved around the line in 2025. Veteran OL who can play multiple positions. Re-signed for 2026 and expressed enthusiasm about staying. May compete at center or guard with Biadasz released.

trade_value.reasoning: Versatile veteran OL on a modest contract. Useful depth piece but not a premium starter. Worth a mid-to-late round pick to a team needing OL flexibility.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed but at a reasonable number. If Washington upgrades at center (Linderbaum linked), Wylie's role could shift to pure depth, making him moveable.

players.name: Brandon Coleman
players.performance_evidence: Mentioned as part of Washington's envisioned starting five OL for the future. Young developmental guard who earned playing time in 2025 with Chris Paul's role fluctuating. Cheap rookie deal.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, cheap developmental guard with starting experience. Low cost makes him a useful roster piece, but limited proven production keeps trade value modest.

players.availability_reasoning: Cheap contract, young, developing. Commanders see him as part of their OL future and wouldn't give him away. But if a team offered a Day 3 pick, they'd listen.

players.name: Odafe Oweh
players.performance_evidence: 17.5 sacks over last 2 seasons combined (10 with BAL in 2024, 7.5 with LAC in 2025). 3 sacks in a single playoff game vs Patriots. 17.3% QB pressure percentage with Chargers. Just signed 4-year, $100M deal ($68M guaranteed). Ascending player who found his best form in Jesse Minter's defense in LA.

trade_value.reasoning: 27-year-old ascending EDGE on a brand-new mega-deal with 4 years of control. Massive guaranteed money makes him untradeable financially. If hypothetically available, worth a mid-1st to a contender given age, production trajectory, and positional value.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed hours ago to a 4-year, $100M contract with $68M guaranteed. The centerpiece of Washington's defensive rebuild under new DC Daronte Jones. Dead cap would be approximately $50M. Zero chance of trade.

players.name: Dorance Armstrong
players.performance_evidence: 2025: 5.5 sacks in just 6 games before season-ending knee injury in Week 7. Was on pace for Pro Bowl numbers. Career-high pass rush grade per PFF in 2024. 3-year, $33M deal ($11M AAV). Has reached 5+ sacks every year since 2021.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality edge rusher but coming off a significant knee injury at age 29 on an expiring contract with $0 guaranteed remaining. The injury risk and contract situation drastically reduce value. Worth a conditional 4th at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Final year of deal, $0 guaranteed remaining, coming off serious knee injury. With Oweh signed plus Chaisson and Omenihu added, Armstrong could be a trade chip if he shows he's healthy. Commanders may also restructure or seek an extension if he proves healthy.
players.injury_notes: Season-ending knee injury in Week 7 of 2025 (vs Dallas). Placed on IR October 21, 2025. Severity unknown but recovery timeline extends into 2026. This is the single biggest factor in his value.

players.name: Daron Payne
players.performance_evidence: 4-year, $90M contract signed March 2023. Career-high 11.5 sacks in 2022 (Pro Bowl). Production has 'flat-lined' since per Spotrac — combined 8 sacks over last 2 seasons. Team-high $28M cap hit in 2026. Extremely durable — 84 consecutive starts before missing NFC Championship Game with knee/finger injuries.

trade_value.reasoning: Productive DT but entering a contract year at $28M cap hit with declining production. Dead cap of $11.2M is manageable. Worth a 3rd-round pick to a contender needing DL help, but the $28M cap hit is a major barrier to trade.

players.availability_reasoning: Team-high $28M cap hit on an expiring deal with $0 guaranteed. Spotrac flagged him as a potential cap casualty with $16.8M in savings if released after the season. Had a $5M March 15th roster bonus deadline. Production hasn't matched the contract since 2022. With Tim Settle signed and Jer'Zhan Newton emerging, the Commanders have interior DL depth.
players.injury_notes: Missed NFC Championship Game with knee/finger injuries. Generally extremely durable throughout career.
players.trade_buzz: Ben Standig of The Athletic predicted Payne could be a salary cap casualty. PFF noted his spot 'could be in jeopardy' as Peters pursues upgrades. With Oweh and new DL additions, Payne's role is evolving.

players.name: Jer'Zhan Newton
players.performance_evidence: 2025: 5.0 sacks and 17 games played — significant improvement over 2.0 sacks as rookie. PFF tackle grade up 13 points to 76.4. Named to NFL 'All-Breakout Team' by Bucky Brooks. 3 sacks, 5 QB hits, 9 tackles in Christmas Day game vs Dallas. Game started 'slowing down' per coaching staff. 4-year, $9.75M rookie deal.

trade_value.reasoning: 23-year-old ascending interior DL on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control. 5 sacks as a second-year 3-tech is excellent production. Young, cost-controlled DTs who can rush the passer are extremely valuable. Worth a 2nd-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Emerging young DT who the Commanders see as a core piece. However, with Payne, Settle, and other DL additions, a team offering a 2nd-round pick could create a conversation. It would take a clear overpay — Washington views Newton as a building block.
players.injury_notes: Foot surgery before rookie year (same injury that dropped him from 1st round). Ankle issues during 2025 (questionable multiple weeks).

players.name: Frankie Luvu
players.performance_evidence: 2024: Career-high 8 sacks, 12 TFL — earned All-Pro recognition and NFL Top 100 (#70). 2025: Regressed to 3 sacks, partly due to being forced into more of an edge rusher role due to injuries. 3-year, $31M contract in final year. New DC Daronte Jones spoke highly of him and plans to use him as a blitzing LB.

trade_value.reasoning: Versatile LB/EDGE hybrid on an expiring deal. Regressed in 2025 but was All-Pro caliber in 2024. New DC may unlock his best role again. Worth a 3rd-round pick to a contender, but expiring contract limits return. Bleacher Report named him the Commanders' top trade asset.

players.availability_reasoning: Bleacher Report explicitly named Luvu the Commanders' 'Top Trade Asset' this offseason. Final year of contract, 29 years old, production dropped from 8 sacks to 3. With Leo Chenal signed ($24.75M deal), Washington has LB depth. A contender could offer a mid-round pick and Washington would listen.
players.character_notes: Fined 6 times over last 4 seasons for various violations including hip-drop tackles (3 violations in 2025), fighting, roughing the passer. Nearly received the first-ever NFL suspension for hip-drop tackles before appeal reduced it to a $100K fine.
players.trade_buzz: Bleacher Report's Alex Ballentine labeled Luvu the team's 'Top Trade Asset.' His sacks dropped from 8 to 3 and 'he's 29 years old and in need of an extension after this season.' Multiple outlets have discussed his trade candidacy.

players.name: Leo Chenal
players.performance_evidence: Signed 3-year, $24.75M deal from Kansas City in March 2026 FA. Known for range, blitzing ability, and versatility. ESPN noted he 'brings range and prowess as a blitzer to Washington' and should fit well in DC Daronte Jones' system.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old versatile LB on a new 3-year deal just signed in free agency. Young, athletic, affordable for his skill set. Just acquired — effectively untradeable in Year 1 due to dead cap and roster need.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed in free agency specifically to fill a major need. High dead cap in Year 1. Washington desperately needed LB help and Chenal was their targeted addition.

players.name: Mike Sainristil
players.performance_evidence: 2024 (rookie): Outstanding — 93 tackles, 14 PD, 2 INT. Key contributor to NFC Championship run. 2025: Major regression — PFF overall grade 51.7 (98th of 114 CBs), worst perimeter CB per PFF. Still led team with 4 INTs and had 85 tackles. Better as a slot/nickel CB than outside. New DC Jones spoke highly of him and plans to deploy him properly.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old CB on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control. The 2024 production shows real talent, but the 2025 regression raises concerns. Best fit is as a nickel CB, which is where Washington plans to use him in 2026. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd based on age, contract, and 2024 film.

players.availability_reasoning: Commanders view Sainristil as a core defensive piece they expect to bounce back. With Amik Robertson signed and Trey Amos returning, Washington has CB depth, but Sainristil fills the nickel role specifically. They'd only move him for a premium offer.

players.name: Trey Amos
players.performance_evidence: 2025 rookie: Won starting job out of camp over veterans. Was the 'best CB on the team' per beat writers before suffering an injury in early November. Dominated in coverage vs KC (1 catch allowed on 3 targets for 6 yards with a PBU). 18 solo tackles in limited games. Called a 'true lock' on the roster by Nicki Jhabvala.

trade_value.reasoning: 23-year-old CB1 on a rookie deal with 4 years of control who was the team's best corner as a rookie before injury. Young outside CBs on cheap deals who flash like this are worth a 2nd-round pick. The injury cut his rookie year short but the tape was excellent.

players.availability_reasoning: Washington's best CB prospect and a building block for the defense. Called a 'true lock' on the roster. Just 23 years old with 4 years of cheap control. Zero chance of being traded.
players.injury_notes: Injured in early November of rookie season, missed remainder of 2025. Severity and recovery timeline not fully detailed but expected back for 2026.

players.name: Amik Robertson
players.performance_evidence: Signed 2-year, $16M deal ($9.3M guaranteed) from Detroit in 2026 FA. 86 career games, 35 starts, 35 career PBU. Started 10 games with Lions in 2025. At least 1 INT in each of last 4 seasons. Provides experienced depth at CB.

trade_value.reasoning: 26-year-old experienced CB on a new 2-year deal. Just signed to fill a need — high dead cap in Year 1 makes trade unlikely. Worth a 3rd-to-4th round pick based on production and contract.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed in free agency. High dead cap in Year 1. Washington specifically targeted CB help and Robertson fills that need. Would only move him for a clear overpay.

players.name: Quan Martin
players.performance_evidence: 2025: 99 tackles (career high) but PFF grade 50.2 (89th of 98 safeties). Coverage grade 41.4. Benched in Week 17 for blown coverage. Was solid in 2024 (87 tackles, 3 FF, 1 INT, started 16 games). Major regression in Year 3. Entering final year of rookie deal.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 2nd-round pick on an expiring deal who regressed badly in 2025. PFF noted he yielded the highest passer rating when targeted (151.8) among safeties. The draft pedigree and one good season (2024) maintain some value, but the 2025 tape is concerning. Worth a 5th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: PFF labeled Martin a trade candidate. Expiring contract, terrible 2025 performance (benched in Week 17), and the Commanders signed Nick Cross (2-year deal) to potentially replace him. Washington may follow the same trade-before-FA playbook they used with Brian Robinson Jr.
players.character_notes: Ejected in Week 16 for throwing a punch during a brawl between Washington's defense and Philadelphia's offense.
players.trade_buzz: PFF's Bradley Locker explicitly suggested Martin 'could be a trade candidate this offseason.' Compared to the Brian Robinson Jr. trade playbook. Cross signing adds competition.

players.name: Nick Cross
players.performance_evidence: Signed 2-year deal worth up to $14M from Indianapolis in 2026 FA. Young safety with starting experience. ESPN noted the Commanders' 'secondary was way too inconsistent last year and missed too many tackles' — Cross was brought in to address that.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old safety on a new 2-year deal just signed in free agency. Just acquired to fill a specific need in the secondary. Dead cap makes Year 1 trade impractical.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed in free agency to address secondary issues. High dead cap in Year 1. Would only be moved for a significant overpay.

players.name: Daron Payne
players.performance_evidence: 4-year, $90M contract signed March 2023. Career-high 11.5 sacks in 2022 (Pro Bowl). Production has flat-lined since — combined 8 sacks over last 2 seasons. Team-high $28M cap hit in 2026. Extremely durable.

trade_value.reasoning: Productive DT entering a contract year at $28M cap hit with declining production. Dead cap of $11.2M is manageable. Worth a 3rd-round pick to a contender, but the massive cap hit limits suitors.

players.availability_reasoning: Team-high $28M cap hit on an expiring deal with no guarantees remaining. Spotrac flagged him as a potential cap casualty. With Tim Settle signed and Newton emerging, Washington has DL depth. A trade would save ~$16.8M in cap space.
players.injury_notes: Missed NFC Championship Game (knee/finger). Generally extremely durable.
players.trade_buzz: Ben Standig of The Athletic predicted Payne could be a salary cap casualty. Spotrac's financial analysis highlighted the $5M March 15th roster bonus as an early deadline for a move.
